name: Tools
description: List of tools available for generating the response. Each tool is uniquely identified by a name ('name' field) and is associated with a description ('description' field) that explains the purpose of the tool and how it works.

value.name: euclidian_distance(A,B)
value.description: This function calculates the distance between two spatial locations. Let A=[x_1,y_1] and B=[x_2,y_2] be the coordinates of two spatial locations. The distance between these two points can be computed as follows: euclidian_distance(A,B) = sqrt((x_2 - x_1)^2 + (y_2 - y1)^2).

name: Scene
description: Description of the scene and its content

value.name: Spatial Locations
value.description: Spatial Locations in the scene where objects can be placed. Each location is associated with a description ('description' field) and is uniquely identified by a name ('name' field) and a spatial position ('position' field). The position is expressed as [x_value, y_value], i.e., in terms of the x coordinate (x_value) and the y coordinate (y_value).

value.name: red_box
value.description: A red box, sealed with a red lid and designed to store objects of various sizes, shapes, and weights.

value.name: yellow_box
value.description: A yellow box, sealed with a yellow lid and designed to store objects of various sizes, shapes, and weights.

value.name: blue_box
value.description: A blue box, sealed with a blue lid and designed to store objects of various sizes, shapes, and weights.

value.name: green_box
value.description: A green box, sealed with a green lid and designed to store objects of various sizes, shapes, and weights.

value.name: Objects
value.description: A complete list of all the objects available in the scene. Each object is associated with a full list of its features ('features' field) and is uniquely identified by a name ('name' field) and a spatial position ('position' field). Each feature in the list is formatted as {'name': 'feature_name', 'value': feature_value}. The value can be either a string or a numerical value. Moreover, each object has its own weight ('weight' field) that depends on its 'color' and 'shape'.

value.name: Agents
value.description: A detailed list of agents in the scene is provided. Each agent is uniquely identified by an ID ('id' field). Additionally, for each agent, a series of details is provided, including the type ('type' field), which can be either 'human' or 'robot'; the position ('position' field); the maximum ('max' field) and minimum ('min' field) velocities ('velocity' field); the workspace amplitude ('workspace_amplitude' field), which may be finite or infinite; and the list of actions the agent can perform ('action' field). Each action is characterized by a name ('name' field) and a duration ('duration' field). The duration field explains how to compute the total duration based on the available data.

actions.name: PickAndPlace
actions.duration: euclidian_distance(object_initial_position,agent_initial_position)/agent_velocity + 5.0 seconds for picking + euclidian_distance(object_initial_position,desired_position)/agent_velocity + 5.0 seconds for placing + 7.0 seconds allotted for extra operations or movements.

actions.name: PickAndPlace
actions.duration: euclidian_distance(object_initial_position,agent_initial_position)/agent_velocity + 5.0 seconds for picking + euclidian_distance(object_initial_position,desired_position)/agent_velocity + 5.0 seconds for placing + 7.0 seconds allotted for extra operations or movements.

actions.name: PickAndPlace
actions.duration: euclidian_distance(object_initial_position,agent_initial_position)/agent_velocity + 5.0 seconds for picking + euclidian_distance(object_initial_position,desired_position)/agent_velocity + 5.0 seconds for placing + 0.0 seconds allotted for extra operations or movements.

actions.name: OpenBox
actions.duration: euclidian_distance(lid_initial_position,agent_initial_position)/agent_velocity + 5.0 seconds for picking + 5.0 seconds for placing + 0.0 seconds allotted for extra operations or movements.

actions.name: CloseBox
actions.duration: euclidian_distance(lid_initial_position,agent_initial_position)/agent_velocity + 5.0 seconds for picking + 5.0 seconds for placing + 0.0 seconds allotted for extra operations or movements.

name: Preferences
description: Preferences of the agents to consider when responding to a request. Each preference can be seen as a soft constraint, meaning its fulfillment is not mandatory.

name: Directives
description: List of rules to consider when responding to a request. Each rule represents a hard constraint, meaning its fulfillment is mandatory.
value: None of the following rules can be violated.
At the start, each box is closed with its lid firmly in place.
At the end, each box must be re-sealed with its lid correctly placed on top.
Each box must contain at least one object of every color. Objects that match the color of the box must be placed first.
Each box must contain at least 3 objects, and at least one of them must be a parallelepiped.
Each box can contain at most 5 objects.
Lids must not be included in the total count of objects placed inside a box.
Every object can be used only once.
Allocate actions among the agents.
Each agent can perform only the actions listed in its 'actions' field.
Robots cannot handle lids.
The human operators’ velocity varies according to the following relationship: max_agent_velocity/[1 + 0.01*(object_weight - 3)]
Before assigning an action to an agent, verify that the involved object is within the agent's workspace.

Actions involving objects outside the workspace cannot be assigned to the agent. An object is considered within an agent's workspace if and only if it satisfies the following condition: euclidian_distance(object_position,agent_position) < agent_workspace_amplitude.
Specify the order in which each action must be performed.
Assign the same order value to all actions that can be performed simultaneously.
An agent CANNOT carry out multiple actions simultaneously.
Set the start and end times of the actions so that every order-n action finishes before any order-(n + 1) action begins, regardless of which agents carry them out.
The agent_velocity MUST NOT exceed the specified maximum value (velocity's 'max' subfield) nor fall below the specified minimum value (velocity's 'min' subfield).

After each action, every agent resets to its starting position. Consequently, distances MUST always be calculated from that initial position, not from the agent’s final location after the previous task.
When specified in the action description, the distance MUST be calculated taking the object’s desired position into account as well.
PROVIDE an explicit, step-by-step explanation of how the timing for each action is computed.
At each step, verify that the proposed actions comply with all given rules.
At each step, verify that the proposed actions comply with all given rules.
If any check fails, make a correction and verify again. Repeat this process until every check is satisfied, including for the final plan.
Each final plan must be enclosed in '<plan>...</plan>' tags when presenting the solution.
Use the tags <plan> and </plan> EXCLUSIVELY to wrap the final plan; they MUST NOT appear anywhere else in your output.
The final plan must be formatted in JSON as follows: PLAN_TEMPLATE
